title: EU economy forecast to slow down amid rising inflation following energy shock
content: This year’s Spring Economic Forecast reflects the impact on the EU economy of the recent outbreak of conflict in the Middle East. In the wake of the conflict’s energy shock that drove up energy commodity prices, the forecast projects weaker economic activity as inflation rises.

title: Integration at Local Level: EUR 77 Million under AMIF Specific Action
content: The Commission has launched a call for expression of interest under a specific Action of the Asylum, Migration and Integration Fund (AMIF) which will allow Member States to support Local and Regional Authorities (LRAs) in implementing integration measures for third-country nationals.

title: The European Skills & VET Week is back
content: You are invited to a new European Skills & VET Week. Throughout this year, the Week will promote valuable exchanges and help shaping a thriving workforce to harness Europe’s competitiveness. Whether you are a policy maker, expert, learner, employer or training provider, join the conversation.

content: 130 policy makers gather to discuss access to excellence.

title: Integration at Local Level: EUR 77 Million under AMIF Specific Action
content: The Commission has launched a call for expression of interest under a specific Action of the Asylum, Migration and Integration Fund, to support integration measures implemented by Local and Regional Authorities in EU Member States, with an indicative overall budget of EUR 77 million.

title: The EU Mission on Adaptation to Climate Change publishes its 2025/26 Activity Report
content: The EU Mission on Adaptation to Climate Change has published its 2026 Activity Report, highlighting continued progress in turning climate adaptation ambitions into concrete action across Europe.
snippet: The EU Mission on Adaptation to Climate Change has published its 2026 Activity Report, highlighting continued progress in turning climate adaptation ambitions into concrete action across Europe.

title: Commission publishes overview of national emergency measures taken to protect consumers and industry
content: The Commission has set up a repository of the different emergency measures taken by national governments [link] since the start of the Middle East conflict at the end of February to ease the impact on consumers.

title: Selection process for European TEN-T Coordinator
content: The European Commission’s Directorate-General for Mobility and Transport has launched the procedure for the selection of a new European TEN-T Coordinator for the North Sea–Baltic European Transport Corridor, with the appointment expected by autumn 2026.

title: New rules bring increased transparency to the short-term rentals sector
content: New EU rules provided by Regulation (EU) 2024/1028, aimed at increasing transparency in the short-term rentals sector are now applying across Europe. Short-term accommodation rentals have grown rapidly across the EU, largely driven by online platforms, and now represent around one quarter of tourist

title: Commission releases €158.9 million to Albania, Montenegro and North Macedonia under EU Growth Plan for the Western Balkans
content: The European Commission has today made available €49 million to Albania, €44.2 million to Montenegro, and €65.7 million to North Macedonia under the Reform and Growth Facility. This follows the third request for payment and the Commission's positive assessment of reform steps implemented in the...

title: From local action to global impact: EU delivers on biodiversity ahead of CBD COP17
content: Protecting biodiversity is increasingly recognised not only as an environmental necessity, but also as an economic and security imperative.
snippet: Protecting biodiversity is increasingly recognised not only as an environmental necessity, but also as an economic and security imperative.

title: CBD COP17: Addressing the biodiversity financing gap
content: Around 140 stakeholders gathered in Brussels to find innovative ideas as to how to scale up private investment in biodiversity.
snippet: Around 140 stakeholders gathered in Brussels to find innovative ideas as to how to scale up private investment in biodiversity.

title: EU expands support for workers at risk of job loss
content: The Council has adopted new rules to expand the European Globalisation Adjustment Fund for Displaced Workers (EGF). For the first time, workers facing imminent job loss due to company restructuring will be eligible for support.

title: Commission sends Statements of Objections to several companies concerning synthetic turf cartels
content: The European Commission has informed several companies active in the synthetic turf sector of its preliminary view that they have breached EU antitrust rules by colluding to distort competition in synthetic turf for sports pitches in the Netherlands and Germany.

title: A jobseeker’s guide to working across Europe
content: Millions of Europeans are crossing borders for better careers, short-term gigs and new adventures with EURES by their side. You can do it, too!
snippet: Millions of Europeans are crossing borders for better careers, short-term gigs and new adventures with EURES by their side. You can do it, too!

title: EU’s new Waste Shipment Regulation and DIWASS digital platform go live
content: The Digital Waste Shipment System (DIWASS) will be key for the EU's circular economy transition and a decisive step towards full digitalisation.
snippet: The Digital Waste Shipment System (DIWASS) will be key for the EU's circular economy transition and a decisive step towards full digitalisation.

title: Commissioner Kos in Montenegro to mark 20th anniversary of restoring independence
content: Today, Commissioner for Enlargement Marta Kos is in Montenegro to mark the 20th anniversary of the restoration of the country’s independence and to reaffirm the EU’s strong support for Montenegro’s EU accession process.

title: Science comes to Town: Taking European research and innovation where it matters most
content: The second edition of "Science Comes to town" was formally launched to bring science closer to citizens across four European cities in 2027.
snippet: The second edition of "Science Comes to town" was formally launched to bring science closer to citizens across four European cities in 2027.

title: Expert group recommendations on making smart charging and demand response easier

title: Celebrating Natura 2000: the world’s largest network of protected natural areas
content: On Natura 2000 Day, we celebrate the EU’s flagship nature network, the largest network of environmentally protected areas in the world
snippet: On Natura 2000 Day, we celebrate the EU’s flagship nature network, the largest network of environmentally protected areas in the world

title: EU Space for Disaster Resilience: SUNSHINE Project Convened Its Third Training Seminar in Romania
content: From 5 to 8 May 2026, the EU-funded SUNSHINE project is holding its third Training Seminar in Timișoara, Romania.
snippet: From 5 to 8 May 2026, the EU-funded SUNSHINE project is holding its third Training Seminar in Timișoara, Romania.

title: Commission welcomes political agreement on implementation of EU-US trade deal
content: The European Commission welcomes the political agreement reached today between the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on two Regulations eliminating tariffs on all US industrial goods and providing preferential market access for certain US agricultural and seafood products.

title: Youth-led movement steps up across Europe to save disappearing pollinators
content: A new cohort of projects backs solutions pushed by youngsters, from urban habitats to DNA-based monitoring.

title: Commission approves €1.3 billion German State aid to support renewable hydrogen production
content: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €1.3 billion German State aid scheme to support the production of renewable hydrogen through the European Hydrogen Bank's “Auctions-as-a-Service” tool for the auction that closed in 2026.

title: Commission approves €288 million German State aid for first-of-a-kind facilities in semiconductor value chain
content: The European Commission has approved €288 million in German State aid to support the setting up two new facilities in the semiconductor supply chain.
snippet: The European Commission has approved €288 million in German State aid to support the setting up two new facilities in the semiconductor supply chain.

title: Strengthening EU farming resilience: study confirms key adaptation pathways
content: A new Commission study confirms climate change as the main driver reshaping EU agriculture and highlights concrete adaptation efforts already underway, offering insights to further strengthen resilience, competitiveness and long-term farm viability.

title: Commission seeks feedback on the functioning of EU crypto-assets rules
content: This consultations aims at gathering feedback from stakeholders and the wider public on the functioning of the EU’s regulatory framework on crypto‑assets
